scenario: CO opens, BB calls. Flop K♠7♥2♣. CO holds A♣K♦. BB checks. You are CO.
question: Categorize your hand strength and name the default plan.
explanation: Top pair top kicker (A♣K♦ on K-7-2) is STRONG VALUE. You're the preflop raiser and in position (CO vs BB) — bet for value across multiple streets; value betting is where most micro profit comes from. You want worse Kx, pairs, and draws to pay you off.

scenario: MP opens, BTN calls. Flop J♦8♠5♥, MP bets, BTN calls. Turn 2♣. MP holds J♠9♣. MP is first to act; BTN has been tight. You are MP.
question: Categorize your hand strength and name the default plan.
explanation: Top pair with a WEAK kicker (J9 on J-8-5) is MEDIUM STRENGTH — showdown value, but dominated by better Jx. You're OOP (MP vs BTN) as the aggressor, and the tight caller's range is firming up. Pot control: check, or thin-value bet only vs weaker players. Don't bloat the pot with a one-pair weak-kicker hand. (If the kicker were an A/K, this would be top pair TOP kicker = Strong — the kicker splits the tiers.)

scenario: BTN opens, BB calls. Flop T♠9♦4♣. BTN holds Q♠J♠. BB checks. You are BTN.
question: Categorize your hand strength and name the default plan.
explanation: This is a DRAW — and a strong one: an open-ended straight draw (any K or 8, 8 outs) + two overcards + a backdoor flush. You're in position (BTN vs BB) as the aggressor. Default: semi-bluff — you have equity if called AND fold equity now, and can barrel many turns. The draw upgrades you from 'nothing yet' to a betting hand; don't check it back.

scenario: CO opens, BB calls. BB is a loose-passive calling station. Flop K♣9♠4♦, CO bets, BB calls. Turn 7♦. CO holds A♥5♥. BB checks. You are CO.
question: Categorize your hand strength and name the default plan.
explanation: Ace-high, no pair, no draw is WEAK. You're IP as the aggressor, but the opponent is a calling station who doesn't fold — so a bluff has no fold equity and you have no showdown value worth betting. Check and give up unless you improve. The PLAYER TYPE drives the line: vs a high-fold reg one stab is fine, but never fire at a station with air.

scenario: A TAG 3-bets preflop, Hero calls with K♠T♠. Flop 6♦Q♣J♠. The 3-bettor bets. Hero is out of position. You are Hero.
question: Categorize your hand strength and name the default plan.
options: Draw — 8 outs (any A or 9) + an overcard, enough equity to continue
explanation: This is a DRAW: any A (4) or any 9 (4) completes a straight = 8 outs (~31% by the river), plus the K overcard adds a few more. Not a weak gutshot — a real open-ended draw. You're OOP as the caller vs a 3-bettor's strong range, so respect it, but 8 outs + overcard is a continue (call, or semi-bluff-raise depending on price/fold equity), not a fold. (Postflop spot only — the preflop call is taken as given.)

scenario: BTN opens, BB calls. Flop K♦Q♠5♣, BB checks, BTN bets, BB calls. Turn 3♠, BB checks, BTN bets, BB calls. River 2♥, BB leads big. BTN holds K♣J♠. You are BTN.
question: Categorize your hand strength and name the default plan.
explanation: Hand strength is DYNAMIC — it shifts with the action. K♣J♠ (top pair, J kicker) was decent on the flop, but a passive player who check-called twice then suddenly leads big on a blank river has a range that beats one pair: KQ (two pair), sets, slowplayed monsters. Your top-pair-decent-kicker demotes to a bluff-catcher, and passive players rarely turn a hand into a bluff. Lean fold to the big lead. The action redefines the tier.

scenario: MP opens, BTN calls. Flop 8♥4♣2♦. MP holds 8♠8♦. BTN checks. You are MP.
question: Categorize your hand strength and name the default plan.
options: Strong value (monster) — build the pot; occasionally slowplay one street on a very dry board
explanation: A set is a STRONG VALUE monster. You're IP as the aggressor on a dry board (8-4-2 rainbow, no draws). Default: bet and build toward stacks. You can occasionally slowplay ONE street here since nothing realistically outdraws you cheaply — but the default is to build the pot, not check it down.

scenario: Villain 4-bets preflop, Hero calls with A♣Q♣. Flop K-8-4 checks through. Turn (blank), Villain bets, Hero calls. River pairs the board; Villain bets. Villain's range is QQ+/AK. Hero is the caller. You are Hero.
question: Categorize your hand strength and name the default plan.
options: Weak vs this range — A-high beats nothing the 4-bettor continues with
explanation: A-Q on K-8-4 is ace-high, and vs a tight 4-bet range (QQ+/AK) it's WEAK — it beats nothing villain bets (KK, AA, AK, QQ all beat or dominate it). Facing the river bet with ace-high, you're a bluff-catcher that catches no bluffs (a tight 4-bettor isn't bluffing here), so it's a fold. (Postflop categorization only: your hand is weak vs this range and should give up — the earlier streets are taken as given.)
